name: Whole Salmon with Dill Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Court bouillon by bringing it to a simmer.
2. To poach the salmon, add the fish to the simmering Court bouillon.
3. Allow 10 minutes of cooking time for each inch of thickness in the salmon.
4. Check for doneness by inserting a metal skewer into the thickest part behind the gill; it should slide in easily.
5. For a six-pound salmon, use a fish poacher or a roasting pan with a rack for easy lifting and presentation.
6. Chill the fish carefully, then unwrap it and gently remove the skin. If the fat is gray, scrape it away to reveal the orange flesh.
7. Cut one half of the fillet from the bones, then separate the flesh from the bones.
8. Arrange cucumber slices on top of the salmon in a pattern resembling fish scales.
9. Slice the remaining lemon and arrange around the fish.
10. Chill well before serving.
11. For the dill sauce, combine sour cream and mayonnaise until smooth.
12. Add grated onion, lemon juice, chopped fresh dill, salt, and pepper; whisk to combine.
13. Serve with cucumber slices and lemon wedges on top of the salmon.
14. The fish can be prepared up to a day in advance and stored tightly covered.
15. The sauce can be made up to two days in advance and refrigerated.
